data.title: Trump's tariff suspension: Behind-the-scenes decision with Commerce and Treasury secretaries, some senior White House officials caught off guard
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Trump announced the suspension of tariff policy last night, causing violent market fluctuations. By compiling media information, the timeline behind the event is as follows: 
At 9:33 a.m. local time, Trump posted on Truth Social, asking everyone to "keep calm", and then added that "now is a good time to buy." 
At the same time, U.S. Commerce Secretary Lutnick received a call from Maros Sefcovic, the EU's top trade official, and the EU approved countermeasures against U.S. steel and aluminum tariffs that day. 
By noon on Wednesday, Lutnick had sat in the Oval Office of the White House with U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessant and began to discuss the latest tariff policy with Trump. 
At 1:18 p.m., Trump announced the decision to suspend tariffs on Truth Social. 
It is reported that this sudden turn even caught some high-level officials off guard. After Trump posted the post, his assistants immediately stood up to discuss how to proceed, and Bessant and White House Press Secretary Levitt quickly held a press conference. 
After announcing the suspension of tariffs, Trump told reporters that he had been considering changing course over the past few days and the final decision "may have been made early this morning." (Wall Street News)

data.title: Ethereum contributor Virgil Griffith released from prison; pardon on the way?
data.description:
Virgil Griffith, a key figure in Ethereum’s early development and known for his work on the Ethereum Name Service (ENS), has been released from prison on parole and transferred to a halfway house, according to updates shared on X on Wednesday, April 9, 2025. Griffith was sentenced to 63 months in prison in 2022 after […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/virgil-griffith-released-from-prison/

data.title: Key trading data: U.S. 10-year Treasury yield fell to 4.259%, MOVE index fell to 128.8294
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to market information, affected by Trump's tariff suspension policy, the US 10-year Treasury yield fell to 4.259%, a drop of 3.07% in the past 24 hours.
In addition, the MOVE index fell to 128.8294, a drop of 7.9% in the past 24 hours.

data.title: A high-leverage whale switched from ETH short to BTC long, making a profit of more than $6 million in 24 hours
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, a trader who went long on BTC with 40x leverage on Hyperliquid has currently earned $4.27 million in unrealized profits. He closed his ETH short position on MUX 11 hours ago and switched to going long on BTC, with a total profit of $6.16 million in 24 hours.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields and MOVE Index Experience Significant Declines
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the yield on the U.S. 10-year Treasury note has fallen to 4.259%, marking a 3.07% decrease over the past 24 hours. This decline is attributed to U.S. President Donald Trump's policy to pause tariffs.

In addition, the MOVE Index, which measures bond market volatility, has dropped to 128.8294, experiencing a 7.9% decrease within the same timeframe.

data.title: sUSD continues to decouple, currently quoted at $0.906
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to market data, sUSD continues to decouple, with the lowest price reaching $0.869 and the current price being $0.906. 
Earlier, Synthetix founder Kain said that the sUSD anchor repair mechanism is in transition, and 90% of ETH has been sold and SNX has been added.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: Trump's tariff suspension confirms the validity of the MOVE indicator again, and the next Bitcoin trend may be different from this time
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: With Trump's tariff suspension policy, the current MOVE index has quickly fallen back to 128.83, below the critical threshold of 140.
Arthur Hayes said that this time verified the effectiveness of the MOVE index. "We will be back soon. Enjoy it while there is still time left in the party. Next time, BTC may not follow stocks into trouble first, but continue to expand."

data.title: MOVE Index Declines Amid Trump's Tariff Suspension
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the MOVE Index has swiftly decreased to 128.83, falling below the critical threshold of 140, following U.S. President Donald Trump's suspension of tariffs.

Arthur Hayes commented on the situation, stating that this development confirms the effectiveness of the MOVE Index. He suggested that while there is still time, people should enjoy the current market conditions. Hayes also speculated that in the future, Bitcoin might not follow stocks into trouble but could instead continue to expand.

data.title: Dormant Ethereum Address Sells Significant Holdings After Two Years
data.description: According to PANews, a previously inactive cryptocurrency address has sold 10,702 ETH after two years of dormancy. The transaction, reported by Onchain Lens, resulted in an exchange for $16.86 million USDC, with an average selling price of $1,576 per ETH. The address still retains 6,336 ETH, which is currently valued at approximately $10.53 million.

data.title: PancakeSwap Faces Controversy Over Governance Proposal
data.description: According to Odaily, PancakeSwap is facing significant controversy ahead of a major governance reform proposal. Eight associated addresses have rapidly acquired and locked 25 million CAKE tokens, representing about half of the total unlocked supply, in an attempt to influence the upcoming governance vote. The proposal, known as 'CAKE Tokenomics 3.0,' aims to eliminate the veCAKE mechanism and voting balance system, introduce a simplified emission model, and establish a 4% annual deflation rate. This move has sparked widespread debate within the community, with Curve's founder and several DeFi projects publicly opposing the proposal due to concerns over potential governance manipulation and long-term trust issues.
